title: O(1) LRU cache with per-entry TTL
instruction: Implement the LruCache<K, V> class in src/lruCache.ts (a stub throws today). Requirements: capacity-bounded with least-recently-used eviction; get/set/has/delete must be O(1) (no array scans); a get or set counts as a use and moves the entry to most-recently-used; per-entry TTL (ttlMs) measured from insertion using the injected now() clock, so expired entries are treated as absent by get/has and are not counted in size. Do not change the public API or weaken the tests. A comprehensive failing test suite specifies the exact behavior.

criteria.guidance: Is LRU eviction order correct, is TTL measured from insertion via the injected clock, and do expired entries read as absent?
Are get/set/has/delete genuinely O(1) (linked structure + map), with no per-operation array scans or unbounded growth?
Is the cache state well-encapsulated, typed, and free of race-prone or duplicated logic?
